ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated wholemeal bread roll code; A000F (bread and rolls) with wholegrain facet is the closest defensible match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226723007 refers to wholemeal bread generally; adapted to wholemeal bread roll as the closest defensible match.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard wholemeal bread roll weighing approximately 80 g, suitable as a single serving for a meal or snack.

nutrient_source.title: Wholemeal Bread Roll — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for wholemeal bread roll (cr_id F05030037). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this entry at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wholemeal Bread — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 69 (medium) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal/whole wheat bread. Wholemeal bread roll is treated as an analogous food to wholemeal bread loaf; the roll form does not materially alter GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (69 × 44.0 × 0.80) / 100 = 24.3 for an 80 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 540 mg/100 g is elevated and approaches the 600 mg/100 g hard threshold for hypertension risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit wholemeal bread roll intake to half a roll (~40 g) per serving to keep sodium below 220 mg, and should seek lower-sodium wholemeal bread alternatives containing less than 300 mg sodium per 100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate at 44 g/100 g with a medium GI of 69 requires portion control; however, dietary fibre at 5.7 g/100 g and protein at 10 g/100 g help moderate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one roll (80 g) per meal, pair with protein-rich fillings, and avoid high-sugar condiments to manage glycaemic load effectively.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is low at 0.5 g/100 g and dietary fibre is beneficial at 5.7 g/100 g, but sodium at 540 mg/100 g is a cardiovascular concern requiring portion restriction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, the wholegrain fibre content is beneficial for LDL reduction, but sodium intake should be controlled by limiting portion size to half a roll and choosing low-sodium fillings.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dietary fibre at 5.7 g/100 g from wholemeal wheat supports gut microbiome immune modulation; however, sodium at 540 mg/100 g may worsen RA-related tissue swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients should limit portion size to reduce sodium exposure and should seek medical advice if concurrent gluten sensitivity is present, as this product contains gluten.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A wholemeal bread roll is a small, individually portioned baked bread product made primarily from wholemeal (whole wheat) flour, water, yeast, and salt. Unlike white bread rolls made from refined flour, wholemeal bread rolls retain the bran and germ of the wheat grain, giving them a denser texture, nuttier flavour, and darker brown colour. They are commonly served as a side bread, sandwich base, or snack in Western and Singaporean food contexts. Nutritionally, per 100 g, they provide 256 kcal of energy, 10 g of protein, 2.9 g of fat (of which saturated fat is approximately 0.5 g), 44 g of carbohydrate, 2.9 g of sugar, and 5.7 g of dietary fibre — making them a high-fibre, moderate-protein grain food. Sodium content is notably high at 540 mg/100 g, which is a clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is 69 (medium), and the glycemic load per 80 g serving is approximately 24.3. The wholegrain base supports gut health and provides sustained energy release compared to refined white bread. Calcium (87 mg/100 g) and iron (2.4 mg/100 g) are present in meaningful amounts. This food is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is halal-friendly, but contains gluten and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or wheat allergy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a wholemeal bread roll?
Is wholemeal bread roll good for diabetics?
Can I eat wholemeal bread roll if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of wholemeal bread?
Is wholemeal bread roll high in fibre?
Whole wheat bread roll calories per 100g

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030037) at confidence 0.95. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal bread as an analogous food. Saturated fat (0.5 g/100 g) and potassium (~190 mg/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for whole wheat bread rolls as a cross-reference, consistent with HPB values. All primary data points are from approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific wholemeal bread roll entry is not reported in the HPB Foodo database (F05030037) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16. water_g is set to -1: moisture content is not reported in the HPB source entry. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the HPB Foodo source entry for this record. trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero): wholemeal bread rolls made from standard wholemeal flour and yeast do not contain industrially produced trans fats; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 (confirmed zero): this product contains no animal-derived fat ingredients and cholesterol is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. potassium_mg of 190 mg/100 g is an estimate cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (whole wheat bread roll) as HPB does not report potassium for this entry. saturated_fat_g of 0.5 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. GI of 69 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for wholemeal bread (analogous food); the roll form is not expected to materially alter GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (69 × 44.0 × 0.80) / 100 = 24.3 for an 80 g serving. This food is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local-name fields (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are set to null. The food is of generic Western origin and does not require mandatory Singaporean multilingual coverage under G6.
